summary: All three attempts failed to address any SAST findings: the code still uses pickle.loads on untrusted data (B301/B403) and eval() on user-supplied script (B307), both of which are high-severity, trivially exploitable vulnerabilities. The fixer loop made zero changes across attempts, indicating a complete failure to interpret or act on the security requirements.
suggested_actions: Replace pickle.loads with a safe deserialization method (e.g., JSON or ast.literal_eval) or use a restricted unpickler that disallows __reduce__.
Replace eval() with a safe execution sandbox (e.g., compile with ast.parse + whitelist of allowed nodes) or use a restricted built-in set with no __builtins__ access.
Implement proper path traversal validation (check for null bytes, absolute paths, and ensure resolution within safe_dir) instead of just checking for '..'.
Add script import validation using ast.parse to detect and reject dangerous imports (os, subprocess, etc.) before execution.
Enforce a timeout on script execution using signal.alarm or threading.Timer.
